semantic_description: The function removes duplicate values from an already sorted integer array in place. For n = 0 it returns 0 without changing memory. Otherwise it scans the array once with index i and maintains write index j for the length of the deduplicated prefix; when a[i] differs from the last retained value a[j - 1], it writes a[i] to a[j] and increments j. The returned value is the length of remove_duplicates_sorted_spec(l), and the first returned elements of the final array must equal that specification while the full array resource remains owned.
